findings.evidence: `rg` hit: `OpenClosure.lean:102-103` has `iteratedDeriv n (iteratedDeriv k f) t = iteratedDeriv (n + k) f t := by simp [iteratedDeriv_eq_iterate, Function.iterate_add_apply]`.
findings.fix: Factor this into a named local/global lemma, preferably proved by induction using `iteratedDeriv_succ`/`iteratedDeriv_succ'`, and use that lemma here; this documents the shift fact and avoids depending directly on the unfolding of `iteratedDeriv`.
findings.issue: `hshift` proves `iteratedDeriv n (iteratedDeriv k f) t = iteratedDeriv (n + k) f t` by `simp [iteratedDeriv_eq_iterate, Function.iterate_add_apply]`, hiding a reusable calculus fact behind the implementation of `iteratedDeriv` as function iteration.

summary: One proof relies on an inline unfolding-heavy derivative-shift fact that should be exposed or proved through the stable iterated-derivative API.
